cases.input: I need help planning an LDR prostate brachytherapy case using I-125 seeds. The patient is 65 years old with intermediate-risk prostate cancer (Gleason 3+4, PSA 9.8, T2a). The prostate volume is 32cc. Please create a preplan with appropriate seed loading, including peripheral loading pattern with a minimum of 100 seeds. The prescription dose should be 145 Gy.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: I-125 prostate preplan peripheral loading 145 Gy intermediate risk

cases.input: For a cervical cancer patient, I need an HDR brachytherapy plan using tandem and ovoids applicator. The tandem length is 6cm, ovoid diameter is 2.5cm. The prescription dose is 5.5 Gy per fraction to point A, delivered in 5 fractions with 2 fractions per day. Please optimize the dwell positions and dwell times to maximize target coverage while respecting OAR constraints.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: HDR tandem ovoid cervical point A 5.5 Gy fractionation dwell optimization

cases.input: Please plan a breast APBI treatment using a MammoSite balloon catheter. The balloon-to-skin distance is 7mm, and the balloon volume is 42cc. The prescription dose is 34 Gy in 10 fractions. I need the dwell positions optimized to provide adequate dose coverage of the PTV while minimizing skin dose.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: MammoSite APBI 34 Gy balloon dwell optimization skin dose

cases.input: I have an endometrial cancer patient who needs vaginal cuff brachytherapy. The patient is post-hysterectomy. Using a vaginal cylinder applicator with 2.5cm diameter, please plan the HDR treatment with a prescription dose of 3 Gy per fraction to 0.5cm depth, delivered in 5 fractions for adjuvant treatment.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: vaginal cuff cylinder 3 Gy adjuvant endometrial HDR

cases.input: For a prostate cancer patient with a large prostate of 75cc, I need help with neoadjuvant hormonal therapy planning followed by brachytherapy. After 3 months of LHRH agonist, the prostate has shrunk to 52cc. Please create an LDR plan using Pd-103 seeds with a prescription dose of 100 Gy.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: Pd-103 LDR prostate neoadjuvant hormonal 100 Gy large prostate

cases.input: Please create an HDR brachytherapy plan for an anal cancer boost. The patient has a 3cm anal canal tumor and has received 45Gy in 25 fractions of external beam. The boost plan should use a single-channel rectal applicator with 3 fractions of 5 Gy each. Optimize dwell positions to cover the tumor while sparing the bladder and rectum.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: anal boost rectal applicator HDR 5 Gy boost OAR optimization

cases.input: I need an HDR plan for a patient with oral tongue cancer receiving interstitial brachytherapy. The implant has 8 needles placed 1cm apart in a single plane. The active length of each needle is 4cm. The prescription dose is 6 Gy per fraction in 3 fractions. Please optimize the dwell positions to achieve a uniform dose distribution across the implant.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: oral tongue interstitial single-plane 8-needle 6 Gy uniform dose

cases.input: For a patient with recurrent endometrial cancer at the vaginal cuff, I need a PDR brachytherapy plan. The prescription dose is equivalent to 60 Gy in 2 Gy fractions using LDR equivalent calculations. The pulse schedule should be 0.5 Gy per hour over 48 hours. Please create the plan using a vaginal cylinder with dwell positions.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: PDR vaginal cuff endometrial recurrent 0.5 Gy pulse equivalent dose

cases.input: Please plan an HDR prostate brachytherapy case using a template-guided approach. The patient has a 28cc prostate with a planned 18 needles. The prescription dose is 9 Gy per fraction in 2 fractions. Optimize the dwell positions across all needles to achieve D90 > 100% and V100 > 95% while keeping the urethral Dmax below 110% of the prescription dose.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: template HDR prostate 9 Gy D90 V100 urethral dose optimization

cases.input: I have a cervical cancer patient with a tandem and ring applicator. The tandem is 6cm with a 15-degree curvature. The ring diameter is 26mm. Please plan the HDR treatment with 4 fractions of 7 Gy each, delivered twice daily. The target is HR-CTV D90 > 87 Gy EQD2, and I need the D2cc of the bladder < 80 Gy EQD2 and rectum < 70 Gy EQD2.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: tandem ring cervical HR-CTV D90 EQD2 OAR D2cc constraint

cases.input: For a head and neck cancer patient, please plan an interstitial brachytherapy boost using the Paris system. The implant consists of 6 parallel needles at 1.8cm spacing with an active length of 5cm. The prescription dose is 20 Gy delivered in 4 fractions. The treatment volume is a 3cm x 2.5cm x 2cm tumor in the buccal mucosa.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: Paris system interstitial head neck buccal 20 Gy 4 fractions needle spacing

cases.input: Please create a prostate brachytherapy preplan for a patient with a prior TURP. The prostate volume is 28cc with a TURP defect in the median lobe. The prescription dose is 145 Gy with I-125 seeds. I need the plan to account for the TURP defect by adjusting seed positions to avoid the defect region while maintaining adequate peripheral dose.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: TURP prostate I-125 preplan defect adjustment 145 Gy

cases.input: For a vulvar cancer patient, I need an HDR interstitial brachytherapy plan. The template has 10 needles placed in a modified Paris configuration around a 4cm tumor on the left labium. The prescription dose is 4 Gy per fraction in 8 fractions. Optimize dwell times to achieve good target coverage while keeping the urethral and rectal doses within tolerance.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: vulvar interstitial modified Paris 4 Gy urethral rectal optimization

cases.input: I need an HDR plan for endoluminal brachytherapy of a bronchial tumor. The patient has a 2cm endobronchial lesion in the right main bronchus. Using a 6 French catheter with 6 dwell positions, plan the treatment with 5 Gy per fraction in 3 fractions. The dose should be prescribed at 1cm from the source axis.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: endoluminal bronchial HDR catheter dwell positions 5 Gy prescription

cases.input: For a gallbladder cancer case, please plan an interstitial HDR brachytherapy treatment. The GTV is approximately 4cm x 3cm x 3cm adjacent to the liver hilum. I plan to place 6 needles through the percutaneous approach. The prescription dose is 6 Gy per fraction in 3 fractions. Optimize dwell positions to cover the GTV while limiting the duodenal D2cc.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: gallbladder interstitial 6 needles 6 Gy GTV duodenal dose optimization

cases.input: Please create a surface brachytherapy plan for a skin cancer on the chest wall. The lesion is 3cm x 2cm, and I am using a custom surface applicator with 4 dwell positions. The prescription dose is 4 Gy per fraction in 8 fractions to 5mm depth. Optimize to provide uniform dose across the treatment area while limiting dose to the underlying lung.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: surface applicator skin 4 Gy 5mm depth uniform dose lung sparing

cases.input: I have a patient with a large rectal tumor (5cm from anal verge, T3N1) who has completed 45Gy EBRT. I need a brachytherapy boost plan using HDR. Place 8 interstitial needles through a transperineal approach around the tumor. Plan with 3 fractions of 5 Gy each. Optimize to cover the tumor while respecting the prostate and bladder dose constraints.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: rectal interstitial boost 8 needles 5 Gy prostate bladder constraint

cases.input: For a patient with urethral cancer, please plan an HDR interstitial brachytherapy treatment. The tumor involves 4cm of the bulbar urethra. Using a template with 8 needles, plan 4 fractions of 4.5 Gy each. The urethra must be at the center of the implant, and the plan must limit the rectal D2cc while maintaining target coverage.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: urethral interstitial 8 needles 4.5 Gy rectal D2cc central urethra

cases.input: I need a combined EBRT plus brachytherapy plan for a patient with locally advanced cervical cancer. The patient will receive 45Gy in 25 fractions to the pelvis followed by HDR brachytherapy. Using a tandem and ovoids applicator, plan 4 fractions of 7 Gy each to point A. Calculate the total EQD2 to point A and the relevant OARs.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: cervical combined EBRT HDR 45Gy tandem ovoid EQD2 point A

cases.input: For a prostate cancer patient with bilateral hip replacements causing artifact, plan an LDR I-125 seed implant. The prostate volume is 38cc. Due to the artifact, CT-based dose calculation may be inaccurate. Please plan using modified seed positions that avoid the regions with severe artifact and discuss the limitations of the plan.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: I-125 prostate hip replacement artifact modified seed plan limitations

cases.input: Please plan an HDR brachytherapy treatment for a patient with nasopharyngeal carcinoma. The patient has residual mucosal disease after 70Gy EBRT. Using an intracavitary applicator, plan 3 fractions of 5 Gy each. The dose must cover the nasopharyngeal mucosa while sparing the brainstem (D2cc < 60 Gy EQD2) and temporal lobes.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: nasopharyngeal intracavitary 5 Gy brainstem temporal lobe OAR

cases.input: I have a pediatric patient with a bladder neck rhabdomyosarcoma, age 6. After 3 cycles of VAC chemotherapy, the residual tumor is 2cm. Please plan a brachytherapy boost using interstitial needles placed under anesthesia. The prescription dose should be 1.5 Gy per fraction twice daily for 5 days (total 15 Gy).
cases.description: Treatment planning test: pediatric bladder rhabdomyosarcoma interstitial boost 1.5 Gy BID

cases.input: For a patient with a penile cancer (2cm lesion on glans), please plan an interstitial HDR brachytherapy treatment. Using 6 needles in a single plane, plan 3 fractions of 6 Gy each. The plan must maintain the urethral dose below the prescription dose and cover the visible tumor with at least 95% of the prescription isodose.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: penile interstitial single plane 6 Gy urethral sparing 95% coverage

cases.input: I need an HDR plan for a patient with a bile duct cancer. The tumor is at the hepatic hilum, and the plan is for percutaneous interstitial brachytherapy. Place 4 needles under CT guidance. The prescription dose is 7.5 Gy per fraction in 2 fractions. The plan must limit the duodenal D2cc to less than 75 Gy EQD2 and the liver V20 to less than 30%.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: biliary interstitial CT-guided 7.5 Gy duodenal liver constraint

cases.input: For a patient with a vaginal melanoma in the upper third, please plan an HDR interstitial brachytherapy treatment. The template has 12 needles in a modified Heyman configuration. The prescription dose is 3.5 Gy per fraction in 10 fractions. Optimize to cover the tumor while sparing the bladder (D2cc < 70 Gy EQD2) and rectum (D2cc < 65 Gy EQD2).
cases.description: Treatment planning test: vaginal melanoma interstitial 12 needles bladder rectum D2cc constraint

cases.input: Please plan a prostate brachytherapy case using stranded I-125 seeds. The prostate volume is 42cc, intermediate-risk disease. The strands are loaded with 10 seeds each at 1cm spacing. Create a preplan with 4 strands placed in the peripheral zone and 2 strands in the transition zone. Prescription dose is 145 Gy.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: stranded I-125 prostate peripheral transition zone 145 Gy

cases.input: For a cervical cancer patient with a tandem and ovoids applicator, I need a plan that maximizes the dose to HR-CTV while keeping the bladder D2cc below 75 Gy EQD2 and rectum D2cc below 65 Gy EQD2. The prescribed dose to point A is 7 Gy per fraction for 4 fractions. Please perform inverse planning optimization.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: cervical inverse planning HR-CTV bladder rectum D2cc EQD2 constraint

cases.input: I need to create a treatment plan for a patient with an anal canal cancer who will receive EBRT 45Gy/25fx followed by brachytherapy boost. The boost plan uses HDR with a single-channel endorectal applicator. Prescribe 3 fractions of 6 Gy each at 0.5cm depth from the applicator surface. Optimize dwell positions for uniform anal canal coverage.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: anal boost endorectal applicator 6 Gy uniform coverage EBRT

cases.input: For a breast APBI case with a SAVI applicator (6 channels), please plan the HDR treatment. The PTV is 55cc, and the skin distance is 8mm. Prescribe 34 Gy in 10 fractions. Optimize dwell times across all 6 channels to achieve PTV D90 > 90% and V90 > 90%, while keeping the skin Dmax < 100% of the prescription dose.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: SAVI 6-channel breast APBI 34 Gy PTV D90 skin Dmax optimization

cases.input: I have a patient with head and neck cancer requiring bilateral neck dissection and brachytherapy boost. The primary tumor in the oropharynx has been treated to 50Gy with IMRT. The brachytherapy boost plan uses 10 interstitial needles placed through a template. Prescribe 3 fractions of 4 Gy each. Optimize to cover the GTV while sparing the carotid arteries.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: oropharyngeal interstitial 10 needles 4 Gy carotid GTV optimization

cases.input: Please create a PDR brachytherapy plan for a cervical cancer patient who cannot undergo HDR treatment due to logistics. The tandem and ovoids are in place. Prescribe a dose equivalent to 70 Gy in 2 Gy fractions to HR-CTV. Use 0.5 Gy pulses every hour over 70 hours. Calculate the required number of pulses and create the plan.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: PDR cervical tandem ovoid 0.5 Gy pulse 70 hours equivalent dose

cases.input: For a patient with a skin cancer on the ear (pinna), please plan a surface brachytherapy treatment using a custom mold applicator. The lesion is 1.5cm x 1cm on the helix. Prescribe 4 Gy per fraction in 8 fractions to 5mm depth. The plan must limit the dose to the underlying cartilage.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: pinna surface brachytherapy custom mold 4 Gy cartilage dose

cases.input: I need an HDR plan for a patient with endometrial cancer and a positive vaginal cuff margin after hysterectomy. The vaginal cylinder is 2.5cm diameter, 7cm length. Prescribe 6 Gy per fraction at 0.5cm depth for 3 fractions for the boost. Then plan adjuvant treatment at 3 Gy per fraction for 3 fractions at 0.5cm depth to the full vaginal length.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: vaginal cuff two-phase boost adjuvant positive margin cylinder HDR

cases.input: For a patient with a recurrent tumor at the gastroesophageal junction, please plan an endoluminal brachytherapy boost. Using a single-channel applicator with 8 dwell positions, prescribe 5 Gy per fraction in 3 fractions. The dose should be prescribed at 1cm from the source axis. Optimize to cover the tumor while sparing the heart and lungs.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: gastroesophageal endoluminal 8 dwell 5 Gy heart lung sparing

cases.input: I need an interstitial brachytherapy plan for a patient with a recurrent tumor in the neck after prior surgery and radiation. The recurrence is a 2cm nodule in the right neck. Using 6 needles, prescribe 4 Gy per fraction in 5 fractions. Optimize to cover the recurrence while limiting the spinal cord Dmax to 40 Gy EQD2 and the carotid artery Dmax to 50 Gy EQD2.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: neck interstitial re-irradiation 6 needles spinal cord carotid dose constraint

cases.input: For a patient with a large 85cc prostate, plan an LDR I-125 seed implant. Due to the large volume, consider using a combination of peripheral and central seed placement. The prescription dose is 145 Gy. Calculate the expected number of seeds needed and create a plan with at least 110 seeds loaded across multiple planes.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: I-125 large prostate 85cc peripheral central loading 145 Gy seed count

cases.input: I have a patient with bilateral synchronous breast cancers requiring bilateral APBI. The left breast has a lumpectomy cavity in the upper outer quadrant (30cc), and the right breast has a cavity in the lower inner quadrant (25cc). Plan both treatments using MammoSite applicators with 34 Gy in 10 fractions each.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: bilateral breast APBI MammoSite 34 Gy synchronous bilateral

cases.input: For a cervical cancer patient with a known kidney transplant on the right side, plan an HDR brachytherapy treatment using tandem and ovoids. The transplanted kidney is in the right iliac fossa. Prescribe 7 Gy x 4 fractions to point A. The plan must limit the right kidney D2cc to less than 20 Gy EQD2 to preserve transplant function.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: cervical HDR kidney transplant right iliac fossa renal protection

cases.input: I need to plan an HDR brachytherapy treatment for a patient with a urethral stricture disease and a 2cm urethral cancer. The tumor is located in the membranous urethra. Using a template with 4 needles, plan 5 fractions of 4 Gy each. The plan must carefully spare the external anal sphincter and the external urethral sphincter.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: membranous urethral 4 needles 4 Gy sphincter sparing

cases.input: For a patient with a tracheal tumor causing 60% luminal obstruction, plan an endoluminal HDR brachytherapy treatment. Using a 4 French catheter with 5 dwell positions, prescribe 7 Gy per fraction in 3 fractions. The dose should be prescribed at 0.5cm from the source axis. Optimize to cover the tumor while limiting the dose to the esophagus and great vessels.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: tracheal endoluminal 4F catheter 7 Gy esophagus great vessel

cases.input: I have a patient with a parotid gland cancer who had a superficial parotidectomy with positive margins. Please plan an HDR brachytherapy boost using 4 interstitial needles placed in the surgical bed. Prescribe 4 Gy per fraction in 5 fractions. Optimize to cover the surgical bed while sparing the facial nerve region and the spinal cord.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: parotid surgical bed positive margin 4 needles facial nerve spinal cord

cases.input: For a patient with a T2N0 glottic laryngeal cancer, plan an interstitial HDR brachytherapy boost after 50Gy EBRT. Using 4 needles placed through a laryngeal template, prescribe 3 fractions of 4 Gy each. The plan must spare the thyroid cartilage and limit the dose to the spinal cord.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: glottic laryngeal interstitial 4 needles thyroid cartilage spinal cord

cases.input: I need a plan for a patient with a liver metastasis from colorectal cancer. The lesion is 3cm in segment VI, and the plan is for CT-guided percutaneous interstitial HDR brachytherapy. Place 3 needles through the liver parenchyma. Prescribe 10 Gy per fraction in 2 fractions. Limit the liver V15 to less than 50% and the risk of radiation-induced liver disease (RILD).
cases.description: Treatment planning test: liver interstitial CT-guided 10 Gy RILD liver V15 colorectal metastasis

cases.input: For a patient with an anal margin cancer (2cm lesion), plan an HDR interstitial brachytherapy boost using 6 needles in a single plane. The prescription dose is 4 Gy per fraction in 5 fractions. Optimize the plan to cover the tumor with the 100% isodose line while keeping the anal sphincter Dmax below the prescription dose.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: anal margin interstitial 6 needles 4 Gy anal sphincter isodose

cases.input: I have a patient with a large vulvar tumor (6cm) requiring interstitial HDR brachytherapy. The template has 16 needles in a modified Heyman pattern. The prescription dose is 3 Gy per fraction in 12 fractions. Optimize to achieve adequate coverage of the large treatment volume while respecting the tolerance of the urethra (D2cc < 65 Gy EQD2) and anus (D2cc < 60 Gy EQD2).
cases.description: Treatment planning test: vulvar large 16 needles 3 Gy urethra anus D2cc EQD2 constraint

cases.input: For a patient with a post-prostatectomy anastomotic recurrence, plan a salvage HDR brachytherapy treatment. The recurrence is at the vesicourethral anastomosis, visible on CT as a 1.5cm nodule. Using 4 interstitial needles, prescribe 6 Gy per fraction in 5 fractions. The plan must cover the anastomotic recurrence while limiting the bladder and rectal doses.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: salvage HDR post-prostatectomy anastomotic recurrence 6 Gy bladder rectal

cases.input: I need an HDR plan for a patient with a recurrent endometrial cancer at the pelvic sidewall. The recurrence is a 3cm nodule adjacent to the right iliac vessels. Using 6 interstitial needles, prescribe 4 Gy per fraction in 6 fractions. The plan must limit the iliac vessel Dmax to 50 Gy EQD2 while covering the recurrence.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: pelvic sidewall recurrent endometrial 6 needles iliac vessel Dmax

cases.input: For a patient with a penile cancer involving both the glans and the distal shaft (3cm length), plan an interstitial HDR brachytherapy treatment. Use 10 needles in two parallel planes, 5 needles per plane. Prescribe 4 Gy per fraction in 7 fractions. The plan must preserve the urethra and maintain penile function.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: penile interstitial 10 needles two planes 4 Gy urethral preservation

cases.input: I have a patient with a cervical cancer and a retroverted uterus. The tandem and ovoids applicator has been placed, but the tandem angle is 30 degrees posterior. Plan the HDR treatment with 4 fractions of 7 Gy each to point A. The posterior tandem orientation may shift the dose distribution. Optimize dwell times to maintain target coverage despite the unusual geometry.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: cervical retroverted uterus tandem posterior angle 7 Gy optimization

cases.input: For a patient with bilateral inguinal lymph node metastases from vulvar cancer, plan a concurrent interstitial HDR brachytherapy treatment for the bilateral inguinal regions. Use 8 needles per side (16 total) placed in a template. Prescribe 3 Gy per fraction in 10 fractions. Optimize to cover the bilateral inguinal nodal volumes while sparing the femoral vessels.
cases.description: Treatment planning test: bilateral inguinal interstitial 16 needles femoral vessel vulvar

cases.input: For a cervical cancer patient with a large exophytic tumor extending to the pelvic sidewall, describe the advanced treatment planning approach using tandem and interstitial needle applicator. How do you determine the optimal needle configuration, dwell positions, and dwell times to achieve adequate tumor coverage while respecting rectal and bladder dose constraints?
cases.description: Advanced planning for complex cervical cancer with interstitial applicator

cases.input: Describe the inverse planning process for prostate HDR brachytherapy using a multi-catheter approach. How do you set up the objective functions and constraints to optimize the plan for prostate coverage while minimizing dose to the urethra, rectum, and bladder? What specific DVH parameters should be optimized, and what are typical optimization convergence issues?
cases.description: Inverse planning for prostate HDR brachytherapy

cases.input: A patient with a vaginal cuff recurrence after prior hysterectomy requires brachytherapy planning using a custom vaginal cylinder. The CT scan shows the cylinder is displaced anteriorly due to a rectocele. Describe the advanced planning techniques needed to account for this displacement, including applicator reconstruction adjustments and dose optimization strategies.
cases.description: Advanced planning for displaced vaginal cylinder

cases.input: For a patient with bilateral hip prostheses requiring interstitial brachytherapy of the prostate, describe how the metal artifacts affect CT-based treatment planning and what advanced techniques can mitigate these issues. Include discussion of metal artifact reduction algorithms, MRI fusion strategies, and how the TPS handles high-density implants.
cases.description: Planning for prostate brachytherapy with bilateral hip prostheses

cases.input: Describe the planning optimization workflow for a complex head and neck brachytherapy case using interstitial implants for a recurrent oral tongue cancer. The patient has had prior surgery with plate and screw hardware in the mandible. How do you optimize the dose distribution to cover the target while avoiding the hardware and sparing the contralateral salivary glands?
cases.description: Advanced planning for head and neck interstitial brachytherapy

cases.input: For an endometrial cancer patient with a bulky cervix and extensive parametrial disease, explain the advanced planning approach for combining EBRT with brachytherapy boost. How do you calculate the combined EQD2 doses to organs at risk when both EBRT and brachytherapy contribute? What specific DVH parameters should be used to evaluate the combined plan?
cases.description: Combined EBRT and brachytherapy planning for advanced endometrial cancer

cases.input: Describe the treatment planning approach for a pediatric patient with a bladder rhabdomyosarcoma requiring brachytherapy. The bladder volume is approximately 80 mL, and the target encompasses a large proportion of the organ. How do you balance target coverage with bladder function preservation, and what modified planning constraints should be used compared to adult protocols?
cases.description: Pediatric bladder brachytherapy planning

cases.input: A prostate cancer patient has a very large prostate gland (120 cc) requiring HDR brachytherapy. Describe the advanced planning approach to achieve adequate dose coverage while managing the increased number of catheters and dwell positions. What specific optimization challenges arise with large prostate volumes, and how do you address catheter convergence and cold spots?
cases.description: Advanced planning for large volume prostate HDR brachytherapy

cases.input: For a cervical cancer patient where MRI-guided brachytherapy is being used, describe the advanced adaptive planning workflow. How do you handle tumor regression between fractions, what are the criteria for plan adaptation versus replanning, and how do you account for changes in applicator-tissue geometry across fractions?
cases.description: Adaptive planning workflow for MRI-guided cervical brachytherapy

cases.input: Describe the treatment planning process for PDR brachytherapy of a cervical cancer patient. How do you determine the pulse dose, pulse interval, and total treatment time to achieve biological equivalence with HDR fractionation? What specific considerations apply to the repair kinetics of cervical tumors versus rectal tissue in PDR scheduling?
cases.description: PDR brachytherapy planning for cervical cancer

cases.input: For a patient with rectal cancer requiring brachytherapy boost after neoadjuvant chemoradiation, describe the advanced planning approach. The tumor is in the mid-rectum at 7 cm from the anal verge. How do you optimize the dose distribution using endoluminal applicators to boost residual tumor while respecting dose limits to surrounding structures?
cases.description: Rectal cancer brachytherapy boost planning

cases.input: A brachytherapy plan is being optimized for a patient with recurrent endometrial cancer in the vaginal apex. Previous surgery left dense fibrosis and adhesions limiting catheter placement. Describe the advanced planning strategies to achieve optimal dose coverage despite these anatomical constraints, including alternative applicator configurations and dwell position modifications.
cases.description: Advanced planning for recurrent endometrial cancer with anatomical constraints

cases.input: Explain the process of creating a patient-specific quality assurance plan for complex brachytherapy treatments. How do you verify that the optimized dose distribution will be accurately delivered? Describe independent dose calculation methods, Monte Carlo verification techniques, and end-to-end testing using anthropomorphic phantoms.
cases.description: Patient-specific QA planning for complex brachytherapy

cases.input: For a brachytherapy case requiring simultaneous optimization of tandem and ring applicator dwell positions with interstitial needles, describe the multi-applicator optimization approach. How does the planning system handle dose contributions from different applicator types simultaneously, and what are the specific challenges in achieving a conformal dose distribution?
cases.description: Multi-applicator optimization for complex brachytherapy

cases.input: Describe the treatment planning considerations for brachytherapy of a urethral cancer involving the prostatic and membranous urethra with a suprapubic catheter in place. How do you optimize dwell positions and times to cover the entire urethral tumor while minimizing dose to the rectum, bladder neck, and external urethral sphincter?
cases.description: Brachytherapy planning for urethral cancer

cases.input: A center is implementing a new treatment planning system for brachytherapy and needs to validate the dose calculation algorithms. Describe the comprehensive commissioning workflow including TG-43 based dose calculation verification, heterogeneity correction testing, applicator library validation, and parallel calculations with the existing system during transition.
cases.description: TPS commissioning for brachytherapy

cases.input: For a patient with advanced vulvar cancer requiring interstitial brachytherapy after surgical resection, the resection bed extends from the mons pubis to the posterior fourchette requiring a complex multi-plane implant. How do you optimize dose distribution to cover the surgical bed while avoiding the urethra, anal sphincter, and contralateral labium?
cases.description: Treatment planning for vulvar cancer interstitial brachytherapy

cases.input: Explain the concept of dose painting in brachytherapy and its clinical application. How can dose painting be used to deliver differential doses to different regions of a tumor based on biological imaging such as PET or DW-MRI? Describe the planning workflow for implementing dose painting and the clinical evidence.
cases.description: Dose painting in brachytherapy using biological imaging

cases.input: A brachytherapy plan needs to account for a patient with a hip replacement that restricts positioning of the treatment delivery device. How does this physical constraint affect the applicator angle, dwell position selection, and dose distribution? What specific planning modifications are needed to maintain target coverage within mechanical limitations?
cases.description: Brachytherapy planning with hip replacement constraints

cases.input: Describe the planning process for HDR brachytherapy boost in squamous cell carcinoma of the anal canal. The tumor extends from 2 cm to 5 cm from the anal verge with internal anal sphincter involvement. How do you optimize the dose distribution using a combination of endoluminal and interstitial applicators to maximize tumor control while preserving sphincter function?
cases.description: HDR brachytherapy boost planning for anal canal cancer

cases.input: For a center treating multiple brachytherapy sites (gynecological, prostate, head and neck), describe the treatment planning standardization approach. How do you create standardized planning templates and optimization objective libraries for each site? What are the criteria for customizing templates for individual patients?
cases.description: Treatment planning standardization across brachytherapy sites

cases.input: Explain the role of Monte Carlo dose calculation in brachytherapy planning. How does Monte Carlo simulation differ from TG-43 based dose calculation, particularly in heterogeneous tissues near bone or air interfaces? What are the computational requirements, accuracy improvements, and clinical scenarios where Monte Carlo provides the most benefit?
cases.description: Monte Carlo dose calculation in brachytherapy

cases.input: A patient with recurrent cancer of the Bartholin gland requires brachytherapy after surgical excision. The wound bed is irregular and extends deep into the ischiorectal fossa. Describe the advanced planning approach for this uncommon scenario, including applicator design considerations, optimization strategy, and plan adequacy evaluation.
cases.description: Brachytherapy planning for Bartholin gland recurrence

cases.input: Describe the planning considerations for using surface applicators in the treatment of non-melanoma skin cancers near the eye. How do you optimize the dose distribution to cover the superficial target while minimizing dose to the lens, cornea, and lacrimal gland? What specific applicator modifications are available for periocular treatment?
cases.description: Surface applicator brachytherapy planning near the eye

cases.input: For a patient with T2 glottic laryngeal cancer treated with definitive radiation who has persistent or recurrent tumor, describe the salvage brachytherapy planning approach. How does post-irradiation anatomy affect applicator placement, and what specific planning modifications are needed for fibrosis, reduced tissue elasticity, and potential cartilage compromise?
cases.description: Salvage brachytherapy planning for recurrent laryngeal cancer

cases.input: Describe the treatment planning workflow for HDR brachytherapy of Stage III endometrial cancer with cervical extension. The plan must incorporate combined EBRT and brachytherapy dose contributions. How do you calculate composite dose to the rectum, sigmoid, and small bowel, and what planning strategies reduce cumulative dose to these organs?
cases.description: Composite dose planning for advanced endometrial cancer

cases.input: A center is developing a brachytherapy planning protocol for pancreatic cancer using intraoperative placement of flexible catheters. Describe the planning approach including CTV definition around the pancreatic tumor bed, how to optimize catheter dwell positions to avoid the duodenum and stomach, and how intraoperative ultrasound guides catheter placement.
cases.description: Intraoperative brachytherapy planning for pancreatic cancer

cases.input: Explain how to create a brachytherapy plan for a patient with bilateral hip replacements requiring HDR treatment for vaginal cancer. The hip prostheses limit applicator insertion angles. How do you work around these limitations using modified applicator approaches, and what dose calculation considerations arise when treating near high-density metal implants?
cases.description: Brachytherapy planning with bilateral hip prostheses

cases.input: Complex HDR plan for 54-year-old woman with 5.2 cm cervical tumor (IIB) with significant anterior bladder component. Tandem 6 cm, ovoids 20 mm. Address asymmetric disease with dwell time optimization, specific OAR constraints, and left parametrial dose escalation.
cases.description: Advanced planning: asymmetric_planning

cases.input: Salvage HDR for 65-year-old man with recurrent prostate cancer post-prostatectomy (PSA 4.2, 2.1 cm retrovesical mass) after prior EBRT 66 Gy 3 years ago. Include applicator selection, cumulative dose constraints, and re-irradiation rationale.
cases.description: Advanced planning: prostate_salvage_HDR

cases.input: Interstitial brachytherapy for 42-year-old woman with 2.8 cm vaginal melanoma (Breslow 3.2 mm) after excision with positive deep margins. Syed-Neblett template, melanoma-specific dose, and organ preservation approach.
cases.description: Advanced planning: vaginal_melanoma_interstitial

cases.input: Endorectal brachytherapy boost for 70-year-old man with residual 1.5 cm lower rectal cancer after neoadjuvant CRT. Custom applicator design, MRI-based target delineation, dose/fractionation, and QA for custom applicator use.
cases.description: Advanced planning: endorectal_boost
